implementation_summary.approach: Kept directMessages.ts as the single public facade (no folder split). Added sealAndWrap, unwrapAndOpen, buildChatRumor, UnsignedRumor type, GIFT_WRAP_KIND, and CHAT_MESSAGE_KIND to directMessages.ts. Rewrote publishDirectMessage to produce kind-1059 via nostr-tools/nip59 wrapEvent. Added dual-listening in ContactChat.tsx: legacy kind-4 subs (unchanged, D9a) plus a new kind-1059 sub with #p: [selfPubkey] filter. ContactChat.tsx sendMessage now uses buildChatRumor + sealAndWrap instead of the old signDirectMessage NIP-04 path.
key_decisions: Used nostr-tools/nip59 wrapEvent directly — no hand-rolled crypto. The library handles ephemeral key per wrap and randomNow() timing automatically.
unwrapAndOpen catches all nip59/nip44 errors and re-throws a generic 'gift wrap decryption failed' message to avoid leaking plaintext or nip44 padding artifacts.
The gift-wrap kind-1059 subscription uses no author filter — correct per NIP-59 since the outer wrap key is ephemeral and not the peer's key.

signDirectMessage is removed entirely (was only called from sendMessage in ContactChat.tsx). buildChatRumor replaces it for pre-publish id computation. publishDirectMessage still has the same external signature.
The kind-1059 ingest dispatches only kind=14 inner rumors to appendMessage. A TODO comment marks where story-07 will add kind=7 dispatch.
cancelled check is placed after the async appendMessage call in the gift-wrap handler to prevent stale state updates after component unmount.
No NIP-04 dual-publish (D9b): the old signDirectMessage NIP-04 outbound path is completely removed.

seam_contracts.sealAndWrap_signature: (rumor: UnsignedRumor, recipientPubkey: string, selfPrivKeyHex: string): Promise<NostrEvent>

nostr_tools_primitives_round_3: finalizeEvent (nostr-tools/pure, tests only for tampered-seal construction)
nostr_tools_primitives_round_4: getEventHash (nostr-tools/pure) — used in Step 5 of unwrapAndOpen to validate the canonical NIP-01 rumor id

remediation.finding: AC-19 thread-isolation bug: handleGiftWrapEvent ingested inner rumors from any sender into the currently-open thread without validating rumor.pubkey against peerPubkeyHex. The kind-1059 subscription has no author filter (NIP-59 ephemeral outer key mandate), so post-unwrap validation is the only isolation barrier.

production_code.change: Added exported pure helper shouldIngestRumor(rumor: UnsignedRumor, peerPubkeyHex: string): boolean returning rumor.pubkey === peerPubkeyHex
Added shouldIngestRumor import and guard 'if (!shouldIngestRumor(rumor, peerPubkeyHex)) return' immediately after unwrapAndOpen call in handleGiftWrapEvent, before any appendMessage call

remediation.cross_protocol_dedup_status: Already present. chatPersistence.appendMessage deduplicates by message.id at line 50 ('if (existing.some((m) => m.id === message.id)) return'). Since inner-rumor id is used as the message id on both the kind-4 and kind-1059 paths, cross-protocol dedup is automatic. No additional code needed.

remediation.finding: unwrapAndOpen delegated to nostr-tools unwrapEvent which performs two NIP-44 decryptions but does NOT verify the seal's schnorr signature nor bind rumor.pubkey to seal.pubkey. An attacker (Mallory) could wrap a rumor claiming Alice's pubkey using their own key — the shouldIngestRumor guard from round-2 would then incorrectly accept the message as coming from Alice.

production_code.change: Replaced 'unwrapEvent' import with 'verifyEvent' from 'nostr-tools/pure'. Replaced unwrapEvent delegation with a manual 4-step unwrap: (1) kind-1059 check, (2) outer wrap decrypt via nip44.v2.decrypt + giftWrap.pubkey as ECDH counterparty, (3) verifyEvent(seal) — rejects tampered/forged seal signatures, (4) inner seal decrypt via seal.pubkey as ECDH counterparty, (5) rumor.pubkey === seal.pubkey binding check. Added SEAL_KIND = 13 module-private constant.

import_paths.nip44.v2.decrypt: nip44 namespace (already imported via 'import { nip04, nip44 } from nostr-tools')
import_paths.nip44.v2.utils.getConversationKey: nip44 namespace (already imported, same surface as existing getDmConversationKey helper)

fix.new_tests: app/tests/unit/directMessages/sealAndWrap.test.ts — 4 new tests in 'unwrapAndOpen — forgery rejection (round-3 security)' describe block
test_names: rejects a forged rumor: Mallory wraps a rumor claiming Alice pubkey to Bob
rejects a tampered seal signature (verifyEvent check)
rejects a non-1059 kind (kind-4 fed directly)
rejects a non-1059 kind (kind-13 seal fed directly)
fix.regression_status: All 10 pre-existing unwrapAndOpen and sealAndWrap tests still pass. Total: 541 → 545.

remediation.finding: unwrapAndOpen did not validate that the inner rumor's id field equals the canonical NIP-01 hash of its fields. A peer could embed an arbitrary id and cause the id-keyed dedup in chatPersistence.appendMessage to behave incorrectly (e.g., suppress a legitimate message that shares the canonical id, or inject a ghost entry under a chosen id). Practical impact is limited: the round-2 sender binding (rumor.pubkey === seal.pubkey, which itself requires a valid seal signature) restricts the attacker to the actual authenticated peer — so id-forgery is at most self-DoS against the victim's dedup table, not a cross-party forgery. Defense-in-depth is the justification for adding the check.

production_code.import_change: Added getEventHash to existing import from 'nostr-tools/pure' (line 4, alongside verifyEvent)
production_code.validation_block: Added Step 5 after the rumor.pubkey === seal.pubkey check: const canonicalId = getEventHash(rumor); if (rumor.id !== canonicalId) throw new Error('rumor id invalid'); — inside the existing try/catch so the generic 'gift wrap decryption failed' message is preserved.

fix.new_tests: app/tests/unit/directMessages/sealAndWrap.test.ts — 1 new test in 'unwrapAndOpen — forgery rejection (round-3 security)' describe block
test_names: rejects a forged rumor id (non-canonical id in inner rumor)
fix.regression_status: All 15 pre-existing sealAndWrap tests still pass. Total: 545 → 546.
